data.title: Morning News Wrap-Up: Tuesday's Biggest Stock Market Stories!
data.description:
Investors looking for the hottest stock market stories on Tuesday are in the right place. TipRanks has assembled a list of the biggest market happenings traders need to know about today below.

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions

  • Delta Air Lines (DAL) stock fell after the airline company failed to impress investors with its Q4 results.
  • JPMorgan Chase (JPM) stock slipped alongside the release of the financial company's latest earnings report.
  • Bank of New York Mellon (BK) stock was down despite strong Q4 EPS and revenue beats.
  • Spotify (SPOT) stock was down after the music streaming company's CEOs revealed plans to counter user boredom.
  • Amgen (AMGN) stock dropped after the pharmaceutical company reported clinical trial results for its obesity drug.
  • Beyond Air (XAIR) stock rocketed after one of the medical company's subsidiaries reached an agreement with XTL Biopharmaceuticals (XLTB).
  • Ares Management (ARES) stock dipped after the alternative asset management company announced it had raised new capital.
  • AlphaTON Capital (ATON) stock surged after the public technology company confirmed a $46 million AI expansion.
  • International Business Machines (IBM) stock declined despite the computing company opening access to quantum chips to new customers.
  • The Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) was up alongside the latest inflation data.
  • Rio Tinto (RIO) stock rallied on news the mining company has assembled bankers to assist with its acquisition of Glencore (GLEN).
  • My Size (MYSZ) stock was on the rise after the AI measurement company revealed plans to monetize its digital data.
  • Meta Platforms (META) stock took a beating after the social media and AI company revealed layoffs.
  • Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) stock rallied alongside bullish price targets from two top analysts.
  • Diageo (DEO) stock dropped on reports the premium spirits company plans to dispose of its Chinese business.
  • Microsoft (MSFT) stock decreased after the company said it would cover the cost of AI data centers.
  • Steam hit a new all-time concurrent player count, and this could spell trouble for Microsoft and Sony (SONY).

Read all of the top stock market stories at TipRanks.
data.author: William White

data.title: Trump Pressures Powell to Slash Rates Following 'Great (LOW!) Inflation numbers'
data.description:
President Trump reiterated his call for Fed Chair Jerome Powell to cut rates on Tuesday after December's inflation data remained steady. The annual growth of both the Consumer Price Index (CPI) and core CPI remained unchanged from November, coming in at 2.7% and 2.6%, respectively.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"JUST OUT: Great (LOW!) Inflation numbers for the USA," Trump said in a Truth Social post on Tuesday. "That means that Jerome 'Too Late' Powell should cut interest rates, MEANINGFULLY!!!"

Rate Cut Odds Drop amid DOJ Investigation of Powell

However, inflation remains stubbornly above the Fed's target of 2%. In addition, the odds of the Fed holding rates steady at the January 28, March 18, and April 29 Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) meetings all ticked higher following the inflation data, according to CME's FedWatch tool.

Powell's term as Fed Chair is set to end in May. Over the weekend, he announced that he was subject to a Department of Justice (DOJ) investigation over the central bank's $2.5 billion renovation of its Washington, D.C., headquarters. In a video statement, he argued the true nature of the investigation centers on the Fed making policy decisions independently instead of adhering to pressure from Trump to lower rates.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Netflix's Warner Bros 'Love Triangle' Casts Shadow on Upbeat Q4 Outlook
data.description:
Wall Street analysts are taking a cautious approach to streaming giant Netflix's (NFLX) shares despite expecting solid fourth-quarter results next Tuesday, January 20th. The caution stems from the company's "love triangle" with Paramount Skydance (PSKY), namely the tussle to acquire key Warner Bros. (WBD) assets.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Heading into the results, TD Cowen analyst John Blackledge slashed his NFLX price target by 19% from $142 per share to $115, despite sticking to his Buy rating on the stock. Blackledge anticipates that Netflix added about 14.2 million paying users in the quarter, helped by the lineup of its original content, such as the science fiction series Stranger Things, and U.S. National Football League games broadcast over the Christmas period.

In addition, the analyst pointed out that TD Cowen's annual survey of advertisers indicated that more brands are buying ads on Netflix. Moreover, the equity research firm's consumer survey also suggested that Netflix remains the number one choice for watching TV in the living room.

Can Netflix's Q4 Ease Deal Pressure?

On the other hand, Benchmark analyst Daniel Kurnos slammed a Hold rating on NFLX despite noting that he expects the latest earnings update to offer "solid" revenue and operating income guidance.

Kurnos offered no new price target but believes that the Q4 results "could conceivably offer a brief respite" from the current merger and acquisition "love triangle." The analyst noted that the new earnings could be a reminder of Netflix's sound fundamentals.

Furthermore, the Benchmark analyst remarked that the acquisition tussle "is bound to get messier before it gets better." Nonetheless, he sees Netflix as "probably poised to be a winner" regardless of whether the streaming giant pulls through with the deal or not.

Similarly, HSBC analyst Mohammed Khallouf has argued that Netflix's current share price does not reflect the strength of its earnings. Khallouf pointed to pressure from the acquisition pursuit and called Netflix's shares a Buy.

Is NFLX a Good Stock to Buy?

Across Wall Street, Netflix's shares continue to hold a Moderate Buy consensus rating from analysts. This is based on 27 Buys, nine Holds, and two Sells assigned by 38 analysts over the past three months.

However, the average NFLX price target of $128.65 implies over 42% upside from current trading levels.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: Steam's New Record Spells Trouble for Microsoft (MSFT) & Sony
data.description:
The latest user data from Steam, private company Valve's PC gaming platform, showed that Microsoft (MSFT) and Sony (SONY) could be in trouble. Steam hit a concurrent player count of more than 42 million users on Jan. 1, marking a new all-time high for the platform. This new record reveals the massive growth in PC gaming and has increased competition for Microsoft's Xbox and Sony's PlayStation in the video game market.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

While PC and console gaming historically haven't overlapped, that gap is closing. Both Xbox and PlayStation started to release their exclusive games on Steam these last few years in an effort to reach more players and increase sales revenue. This has been a solid strategy to date and likely hasn't hampered console sales due to the difference in PC and console gaming audiences.

Here's the thing: Valve is about to challenge Microsoft and Sony's positions in the video game console market. The company is set to revive its Steam Machine, which is a Linux-based computer designed to compete in the console market. The price and release date of the Steam Machine are unknown, but it could erode Xbox and PlayStation's holds on the console gaming market while bringing more players to Steam.

Microsoft & Sony Stock Movements Today

Microsoft stock was down nearly 1% on Tuesday, extending a 2.12% year-to-date slip. Even so, the stock has rallied 14.8% over the past 12 months.

Sony stock was down 3.22% on Tuesday, building on a 4.8% drop year-to-date. Despite the dip, SONY shares were still up 26.66% over the past 12 months.
data.author: William White

data.title: Goldman Sachs Names 3 Top Software Buys — and Two Stocks to Avoid
data.description:
Top Goldman Sachs (GS) analyst Gabriela Borges has begun coverage of the U.S. software sector, telling investors that AI is entering a "decade of agentic workflow." The firm named Microsoft (MSFT), Oracle (ORCL), and ServiceNow (NOW) as its top Buy-rated stocks, while warning that Adobe (ADBE) and Datadog (DDOG) may face pressure as not all companies are set to benefit equally.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Goldman Sachs' Top Software Picks

  • Goldman's top pick is Microsoft, which it calls its highest-conviction idea. The firm sees upside to Azure revenue in 2026, supported by Microsoft's scale and a disciplined focus on growing the business while protecting margins.
  • Oracle also earned a Buy rating, with Goldman pointing to improving visibility on spending and a likely rebound in profit growth by 2026. The bank is positive on Oracle's position in AI-driven cloud workloads and expects growth to pick up as new data center capacity comes online over the next year.
  • The third Buy-rated stock is ServiceNow, which Goldman sees as a key winner as value shifts toward the "agent orchestration" layer of software. The firm believes ServiceNow's workflow platform and AI tools put it in a strong position to sustain solid growth, while expanding into areas such as CRM, ERP, and human capital management.

Why Goldman Turns Cautious on Adobe and Datadog

However, Goldman Sachs is less positive on Adobe and Datadog, assigning Sell ratings to both names.

  • For Adobe, the firm warned that most new growth in the creative software market is happening at the lower end, where competition is rising and pricing pressure is increasing. It also noted that Adobe faces challenges in attracting new users, which could limit its ability to sustain double-digit growth.
  • Goldman is also bearish on Datadog, warning that tougher competition could weigh on growth. Borges said that while Datadog remains a strong observability platform, competition is expected to intensify in 2026 as customers focus more on cutting cloud monitoring costs. She added that rising AI-driven data volumes and pressure from rivals could slow revenue growth and weigh on the stock's valuation.

Wall Street's Take on These 5 Software Stocks

data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: Trump's Iran Tariff Move Strains U.S.-China Trade Truce
data.description:
On Monday, President Trump announced a 25% tariff on all countries that transact business with Iran in response to its treatment of anti-regime protestors. That could complicate the U.S.-China trade truce.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

"China firmly opposes any illicit unilateral sanctions and long-arm jurisdiction, and will take all necessary measures to safeguard its legitimate rights and interests," said Chinese Embassy spokesperson Liu Pengyu in an X post following the announcement.

China's Iran Trade Exposure Raises Tariff Uncertainty

China is Iran's largest trading partner and purchased over 80% of its oil exports in 2025, according to data firm Kpler. Iran's other major trading partners include India, Russia, and Turkey.

Trump didn't immediately specify when the secondary tariffs would take effect or if the 25% rate would be stacked on top of China's existing tariff rate of 45% for most goods. Last year, he imposed a similar tariff hike on India for buying Russian oil.

data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Diageo Stock (DEO) Falls Flat as it Eyes Up Disposal of Chinese Business
data.description:
Shares in drinks giant Diageo (DEO) fell flat today on reports that it is eyeing up a disposal of its business in China.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Diageo Taps Sale Interest

According to a report on Bloomberg, the Guinness and Johnnie Walker whisky maker is reportedly looking at options including its majority stake in the publicly listed Sichuan Swellfun Co.

It is understood that Diageo has hired investment banks Goldman Sachs (GS) and UBS (UBS) to review its assets in China. It has already tested, according to Bloomberg, the appetite for any deal among local brewing peers and private equity firms.

The group lists its "principal activities" in Greater China as "distilling and warehousing", marketing Chinese whisky and white spirits. Diageo, which also sells some of its international brands in China, owns one distillery in the country and holds just over 63% of Sichuan Swellfun Co.

In Diageo's last full financial year, it saw its sales volumes in Greater China rise 8.4% on an organic basis. However, net sales fell 9% organically.

In its annual report for the fiscal year, Diageo pointed to "challenging economic conditions" and said it had shifted its portfolio in China towards white spirits and lower-aged malts. The group also cut its marketing investment in China during the year.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Trade Mixed on December Inflation Reading
data.description:
U.S. stock futures were mixed on Tuesday morning on a lower-than-expected December inflation reading. Notably, the December 2025 consumer price index (CPI) report revealed that core CPI, which excludes food and energy, rose 0.2% on a monthly basis, below the 0.3% Dow Jones estimate. Meanwhile, headline inflation increased 0.3% on a monthly basis and 2.7% at an annual rate in December. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) and the S&P 500 (SPX) were up 0.07% and 0.04%, respectively, while futures tied to the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.10% at 8:57 a.m. EST on January 13.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Monday's regular trading, all three major indexes closed in the green, with the S&P 500 rising 0.16%, the Dow gaining 0.17%, and the Nasdaq Composite advancing 0.26%.

Turning to trending stocks, JPMorgan Chase (JPM) reported better-than-expected Q4 2025 earnings, driven by strength in the trading business. Shares of Intel (INTC) and Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) rose 3.4% and 2.7% in Tuesday's pre-market trading after KeyBanc upgraded both chipmakers to Buy, citing memory cycle and robust data center demand.

Meanwhile, Delta Air Lines (DAL) stock fell about 4% after reporting mixed Q4 2025 results. In contrast, shares of L3Harris Technologies (LHX) jumped more than 12% in pre-market trading after the defense contractor announced that it would pursue an initial public offering (IPO) of its missile solutions business in the second half of 2026 through a partnership with the U.S. Defense Department.

data.title: AMD and Intel: Top KeyBanc Analyst Turns Up the Heat on These Two Chip Stocks
data.description:
KeyBanc Capital Markets has become more positive on Advanced Micro Devices (AMD) and Intel (INTC), pointing to stronger demand from data centers and cloud customers. After recent checks in Asia, top analyst John Vinh upgraded both stocks to Overweight. The 5-star analyst said tight memory supply and steady spending by big cloud firms are starting to support demand, even as some consumer markets remain soft.

Claim 70% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Why KeyBanc Is More Confident on AMD

For AMD, the analyst raised earnings forecasts for the next two years. KeyBanc now expects AMD to earn $4.01 per share in 2025 and $7.93 in 2026, both higher than earlier estimates.

The analyst said demand from large cloud customers remains solid, even as shortages in memory chips create mixed signals across end markets like PCs and smartphones. While AMD's cloud processor deployments were flat month-over-month, KeyBanc sees the broader data center trend as supportive over time.

Overall, the analyst sees AMD as a key winner as cloud and AI spending continues to grow. He upgraded the rating from Sector Weight to Overweight with a price target of $270.00, implying roughly 30% upside from current levels. 

Intel Got a Boost from New Server Chips

KeyBanc also upgraded Intel to Overweight, pointing to strong adoption of its latest server processors by major cloud providers. According to the firm's cloud tracking data, Intel saw solid growth in newer platforms such as Granite Rapids, Emerald Rapids, and Sapphire Rapids, particularly at Amazon's (AMZN) cloud unit, Amazon Web Services.

Based on this trend, the analyst raised Intel's profit outlook and now expects 2026 earnings of $0.66 per share, above prior forecasts.

KeyBanc said this shows Intel's data center business may be starting to stabilize after a tough stretch, helped by renewed interest in its latest products. The firm set a price target of $60.00, implying roughly 50% upside from current levels. 

Which Chip Stock Is the Better Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, out of the two stocks mentioned above, AMD stands out with a Strong Buy consensus from analysts. AMD stock carries an average price target of $283.90, implying roughly 37% upside from current levels.

By contrast, Intel has a Hold rating from analysts. INTC stock average price target of $40.64 suggests about 8% downside, pointing to more limited near-term upside compared with AMD.
data.author: Shalu Saraf
